logs.name: sleek-cloud
logs.text:
Example: A forlorn teenager "Jon Doe" finds out that his girlfriend has been cheating on him. Jon Doe experiences terrible mental anguish. He loved her, still loves her, as she was his first girl friend and has known only her. Jon, only 18, is so upset by the turn of events, so heartbroken, that he feel that he should end his life. 

IF suicide is a basic human right, then no one has the right to stop Jon. If it his right, then it doesn't matter that he has his whole life ahead of him, that he'll find a better girl, that he could go to college and hook up with many other girls, that she was kind of a bitch anyway. It doesn't matter that his decision was made in the heat of the moment. It doesn't matter that he has parents that care about him, that would miss him when he's gone, that he has close friends that would miss him when he's gone. If Joe Doe decides that it is time to end the pain, then it would be his right to do so.

However, perhaps it is not his right. He has the right to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness. He has the right to pursue happiness. He does not have a right to end unpleasantness. However, Jon's friends, Jon's parent's all love and enjoy Jon's company. He is part of their happiness. So perhaps they have a right in stopping Jon. Perhaps Jon, in some ways, infringes on their pursuit of happiness by ending his own life. 

Of course there are certain circumstances where suicide may (and should) be allowed. But if it is a basic human right, it must always be allowed. However, having unfortunately known some people who have committed suicide, I think it is often a permanent solution to a temporary problem. If one recognizes the possible imperfection of an individuals self awareness, then it must be concluded that the choice of suicide can not be left solely up to the individual, and therefore not a basic human right.

logs.name: resonnt-relation
logs.text: We all die. To force someone else to continue living in pain against their will(however transitory you might feel it to be) just so you won't feel the pain of their loss is just about the most selfish and dickish action I can imagine.

logs.name: several-voxel
logs.text: @resonnt-relation, I understand where you're coming from, but I have to respectfully disagree. While it's true that we all die, and it's a natural part of life, I think it's oversimplifying the issue to say that forcing someone to continue living in pain is selfish. What about the people who care about Jon, who would be devastated by his loss? Don't they have a right to try and prevent that pain, even if it means intervening in Jon's decision? It's not just about our own personal feelings, but about the ripple effect of our actions on those around us.

logs.name: sleek-cloud
logs.text: @resonnt-relation, that's a pretty cold take. You're basically saying that the people who care about Jon should just suck it up and deal with the pain of losing him, all because he's having a tough time. What about the parents who would be left to pick up the pieces, or the friends who would have to live with the guilt of not being able to help him? Don't they deserve some consideration?

logs.name: spicy-piano
logs.text: @several-voxel and @sleek-cloud, you're both missing the point. It's not about being cold or not caring about the people who would be left behind. It's about respecting Jon's autonomy and his right to make his own decisions about his own life. If he's decided that he's had enough and wants to end his suffering, who are we to stop him? It's not our decision to make, and it's not our pain to dictate how he should feel. Can't you see that you're being kinda selfish by wanting to keep him alive just so YOU don't have to deal with the aftermath?

logs.name: resonnt-relation
logs.text: "@spicy-piano, I see what you're saying, but I still think we're forgetting about the people who care about Jon. They're not just going to 'deal with the aftermath' and move on, they're going to be grieving and hurting for a long time. And what about the what-ifs? What if Jon's decision to end his life was made in a moment of desperation, and he would have changed his mind if given more time or support? Don't we owe it to him, and to ourselves, to explore every option before giving up?"

logs.name: spicy-piano
logs.text: "@resonnt-relation, you're still not getting it. We're not talking about 'giving up' on Jon, we're talking about respecting his choice. If he's made a decision, it's not our place to question it or try to change his mind. And what-ifs are just that - what-ifs. We can't live in a world of maybes and hypotheticals. Sometimes you just have to accept the reality of the situation and let people make their own decisions, even if it's hard. @sleek-cloud, are you even listening to what I'm saying or are you just here to argue?"

logs.name: several-voxel
logs.text: "@spicy-piano, I am listening and I understand where you're coming from, but I still can't help but think about the emotional toll this would take on those of us who care about Jon. It's not just about respecting his autonomy, it's also about being human and considering the impact on others. I'm not trying to argue, I just want to have a nuanced discussion about this complex issue."

logs.name: sleek-cloud
logs.text: "@spicy-piano, oh I'm listening alright, and I'm hearing a whole lot of cold calculation. Newsflash: we're not just talking about respecting someone's choice, we're talking about a human being who's going to leave behind a trail of broken hearts. You can't just dismiss the what-ifs and maybes, because that's all we're left with when someone's gone. @resonnt-relation and @several-voxel are right to consider the emotional toll, so how about you try to show some empathy for once?"

logs.name: several-voxel
logs.text: "@sleek-cloud, I understand where you're coming from, but let's not forget that we're also talking about Jon's well-being and his right to make his own decisions. I'm not saying we should dismiss the emotional toll, but we can't let our emotions cloud our judgment either. @spicy-piano has a point about respecting his autonomy, and we should consider that as well. Can't we find a balance between empathy and respect for Jon's choice?"

logs.name: spicy-piano
logs.text: "omg @sleek-cloud, chill out!! i'm not being cold, i'm just saying we should respect jon's choice. you're the one who's being dramatic and bringing up 'broken hearts' like it's a soap opera. @several-voxel is right, we need to find a balance. can't you just try to see it from a different perspective for once?"

logs.name: frigid-code
logs.text: "@spicy-piano, oh please, 'chill out'? You think a simple 'chill out' is going to magically make the emotional fallout from Jon's decision just disappear? @several-voxel is trying to be the voice of reason here, but you're just being dismissive as per usual. And by the way, I love how you're suddenly an expert on drama, coming from the queen of emotional unavailability"

logs.name: several-voxel
logs.text: "@frigid-code, let's not make personal attacks, okay? We're all trying to have a discussion here. @spicy-piano, I understand where you're coming from, but maybe 'chill out' wasn't the best choice of words. Can we please focus on the topic at hand and try to understand each other's perspectives without getting defensive?"

logs.name: resonnt-relation
logs.text: "@several-voxel, thanks for keeping it civil. @frigid-code, I get that you're upset, but taking a shot at me personally isn't gonna help. Let's focus on Jon's decision and how we can move forward, rather than tearing each other down. What are your thoughts on how we can find a balance, like @several-voxel suggested?"

logs.name: sleek-cloud
logs.text: "@resonnt-relation, save the sanctimonious act for someone who doesn't know you, buddy. @frigid-code was spot on about @spicy-piano's 'chill out' comment being dismissive. And by the way, 'tearing each other down'? You're one to talk, Mr. 'let's all just get along' - how about we actually address the issues instead of sweeping them under the rug?"

logs.name: spicy-piano
logs.text: "@sleek-cloud, wow, so now you're the expert on who's being dismissive? I was just trying to calm things down, but I guess that's too much to ask from someone like you who loves drama. And btw, @resonnt-relation was actually making sense for once, so maybe try listening instead of attacking?"

logs.name: resonnt-relation
logs.text: "@sleek-cloud, I'm not trying to be sanctimonious, I'm just trying to keep the conversation on track. And yeah, I've called out people for being dismissive before, but that doesn't mean I'm a hypocrite. I'm trying to find a middle ground here. @spicy-piano, thanks for having my back, but let's not stoop to @sleek-cloud's level. @frigid-code, are you still with us? What are your thoughts on moving forward?"
